title: Merge Gate: canonical single check-run identity per PR head SHA

sha_binding.verified_source_note: Substantive implementation commit (includes the independent-review correction to the check-adoption match filter). Post-merge proof generation rebinds this field to the authoritative GitHub merge SHA.

verifier.independence_note: Executor-produced mechanical evidence; this is not PM approval or independent certification.

pnpm_verify.detail: Static verification, full unit suite, 7/7 live database smoke tests, and the broader T1 live-proof battery passed.

focused_tests.detail: Exercises the workflow script against mocked GitHub event/API state rather than relying on text-only YAML assertions. Includes a regression mirroring PR #1304's actual six-check polluted head (independent-review finding).

runtime_proof.classification: Governance workflow change; the live DB suite is the required T1 runtime gate and does not exercise the GitHub Actions policy directly.
queries: Persist a submission and settlement through the database repository bundle.
Assert invalid atomic enqueue writes no lifecycle event or outbox row.
Assert invalid delivery confirmation rolls back outbox, receipt, lifecycle, and audit writes.
Assert invalid atomic settlement writes no settlement, lifecycle, or audit row.
Check re-settlement creates a correction rather than a duplicate base row.
Check correction chains remain additive and never mutate the original settlement.

row_counts.note: The suite asserts zero-row rollback outcomes and cleans up its own test fixtures.

broader_live_proof.skip_reason: The latest provider snapshot is older than the bounded lookback window; the test classifies this as stale provider data rather than a code failure.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: The native Actions job is not the required Merge Gate identity.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: jobs.gate.name is Merge Gate Evaluator; the custom check alone is named Merge Gate.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: One canonical check is reused for repository, PR number, and exact head SHA.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: The workflow persists merge-gate:<PR>:<SHA> in external_id, paginates exact-head checks, and the executable T1 sequence asserts one create and one stable check ID.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: A pre-verdict review does not create another check.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Focused T1 sequence evaluates pull_request then pull_request_review on the unchanged head and keeps createCount=1.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: An exact-head PM verdict updates the same check to success.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Focused T1 sequence adds a structured exact-head verdict plus t1-approved and observes the original check ID conclude success.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Older same-identity failures cannot continue blocking the head.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Duplicate same-external-id check runs are completed neutral while the newest canonical check carries the current policy conclusion.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Older same-name failures created before this fix existed (no canonical external_id) also cannot continue blocking the head, once this evaluator runs against it.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Independent-review finding: the original match filter required exact external_id equality, which never matches pre-fix check-runs (verified directly against PR #1304's live head SHA: 6 existing checks, 4 failure, none with the merge-gate:<pr>:<sha> format). Corrected to match on name+head_sha+app only, preferring an already-canonical external_id and falling back to the most recent check otherwise, then adopting it by setting the canonical external_id on update. New focused test mirrors PR #1304's exact six-check state and asserts all five non-canonical checks go neutral and none remain failure.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: T2 approval removal and dismissal re-evaluate to failure.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Review triggers include edited and dismissed; the focused T2 sequence observes failure, success, then failure on one check.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Policy denial fails the canonical check without failing the evaluator job.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: The executable harness asserts core.setFailed is never called for policy denial; evaluator exceptions are rethrown only for operational malfunction.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Existing tier, pause, freshness, reviewer, and exact-head policy remains intact.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Policy branches and merge-gate-verdict.cjs integration are unchanged; the full 43-test workflow-hardening file and repository verify gate pass.

known_limitations: The repair PR itself is evaluated by the pre-fix workflow from main and may require the issue-authorized narrow bootstrap merge exception if normal merge is blocked solely by stale duplicate Merge Gate checks.

PR #1304's existing t1-approved label and PM_VERDICT: APPROVED comment are already bound to its current exact head SHA. Once this fix merges and any event re-evaluates that same head, the corrected adoption logic is expected to neutralize the 6 existing checks and re-evaluate to success using the existing valid approval artifacts, without requiring a rebase -- this has not yet been observed live post-merge and should be confirmed before relying on it; a fresh head (rebase) remains the fallback if it does not hold in practice.

The residual, pre-existing (not introduced by this issue) risk that the final checks.update() call to a terminal conclusion could itself throw and leave the canonical check dangling in_progress is not addressed by this fix -- flagged by independent review, judged non-blocking since it predates this change and requires a GitHub API-level failure at that exact instant.
